sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Landstar System Inc (NASDAQ:LSTR) Posts Mixed Q4 2025 Results with Revenue Miss, EPS Beat
feed.summary: Landstar System Inc (NASDAQ:LSTR) reported mixed financial results for Q4 2025, with revenue of $1.17 billion falling below analyst estimates, while non-GAAP EPS of $1.24 exceeded expectations. The market reacted with a slight decline in LSTR's stock price, weighing the earnings beat against the revenue shortfall. Analysts project a rebound in Q1 2026, with full-year 2026 estimates of $5.12 billion in revenue and $5.56 EPS.

feed.title: Hexcel Corp. (HXL) Declares $0.18 Quarterly Dividend; 0.9% Yield
feed.summary: Hexcel Corp. (NYSE: HXL) has announced a quarterly dividend of $0.18 per share, totaling an annualized dividend of $0.72. This dividend offers an annual yield of 0.9 percent and will be paid on February 17, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 9, 2026. The ex-dividend date is set for February 6, 2026.

feed.title: Lockheed Martin to quadruple THAAD missile interceptor production under Pentagon deal
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin has signed a framework agreement with the Pentagon to increase production of Terminal High Altitude Area Defense (THAAD) missile interceptors from 96 to 400 per year. This expansion addresses Pentagon concerns about missile threats and potential interceptor shortages, especially given current deployments in the Middle East and Israel. The deal awaits Congressional funding approval, and Lockheed Martin has already broken ground on a new facility to support the anticipated production ramp-up.

feed.title: Smith, director at Teledyne, sells $2.48 million in stock
feed.summary: Michael T. Smith, a director at Teledyne Technologies, recently sold 4,000 shares of the company's common stock for $2.48 million. These sales occurred on January 28 and 29, 2026, at a price of $621 per share. Following these transactions, Smith directly holds 47,128 shares and his wife owns 200 shares.

feed.title: Teledyne Technologies (NYSE:TDY) Director Michael Smith Sells 2,469 Shares
feed.summary: Teledyne Technologies Director Michael Smith sold 2,469 shares of the company's stock for over $1.5 million on January 29th, reducing his holdings by 4.96%. The sale occurred after Teledyne beat quarterly earnings expectations and provided strong FY2026 guidance. The stock is currently trading around $622, has a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating, and is heavily owned by institutional investors.

feed.title: Top Performing Leveraged/Inverse ETFs: 01/25/2026
feed.summary: This article lists the top 10 performing leveraged and inverse ETFs for the week ending January 25, 2026. The top performers include natural gas, gold, and silver ETFs, as well as leveraged ETFs for AMD and Ether. Explanations for their strong performance, such as weather shifts, safe-haven demand, or company-specific news, are provided for each.

feed.title: First Bancorp (NASDAQ:FBNC) Insider Sells $143,150.00 in Stock
feed.summary: First Bancorp (NASDAQ:FBNC) Director Mary Clara Capel recently sold 2,500 shares of the company's stock for $143,150, reducing her stake by 8.19%. The company reported strong Q4 EPS of $1.19, beating estimates, but revenue fell short; however, it raised its quarterly dividend to $0.24 per share, marking a positive change in its dividend policy. Analysts currently rate First Bancorp a "Moderate Buy" with a consensus price target of $58.00.

feed.title: Palo Alto Networks, Inc. completed the acquisition of Chronosphere, Inc. from a group of shareholders.
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks, Inc. has finalized its acquisition of Chronosphere, Inc. from a group of shareholders. The deal, valued at $3.4 billion with a cash consideration and replacement equity awards of $3.35 billion, was completed on January 29, 2026. Chronosphere, an observability platform provider with over $160 million in annual recurring revenue as of September 2025, will see its team join Palo Alto Networks.

feed.title: NextEra bets on gas as data center pipeline remains steady at about 15 GW
feed.summary: NextEra Energy is focusing on natural gas to power future data centers, aiming for 15 GW of new generation by 2035, with 6 GW from gas-fired resources. The company's CEO, John Ketchum, stated intentions to potentially double this goal and has recently acquired gas infrastructure assets to support this strategy. While investing heavily in gas, NextEra also continues to expand its renewables and storage backlog, securing solar panels through 2029.

feed.title: Lockheed Martin transfers $943 million in new pension buyout while lawsuit remains pending
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin has transferred $943 million in a new pension buyout, even as a related lawsuit continues. The move was reported by Rob Kozlowski on January 29, 2026. This action highlights ongoing financial maneuvering by the defense contractor concerning its pension obligations.

feed.title: W.W. Grainger, Inc. Plans Quarterly Dividend of $2.26 (NYSE:GWW)
feed.summary: W.W. Grainger, Inc. has declared a quarterly dividend of $2.26 per share, maintaining a consistent track record of dividend increases for 55 consecutive years with a low payout ratio of approximately 20.3%. This announcement follows solid financial results including a beat on EPS and revenue growth, and analysts project continued strong earnings per share for the company.

feed.title: Tractor Supply Q4 Sales Rise 3% but Miss Expectations
feed.summary: Tractor Supply's Q4 sales increased by 3.3% to $3.9 billion but fell short of expectations due to a shift in consumer spending where discretionary purchases moderated. Despite this, the company saw a full-year net sales increase of 4.3% to $15.52 billion and plans to open approximately 100 new stores in 2026, forecasting continued sales growth. Essential categories remained resilient, and the retailer focused on expanding its presence in the farm and ranch channel.

feed.title: Enphase Energy to Report Q4 Earnings: Here's What to Expect
feed.summary: Enphase Energy (ENPH) is scheduled to release its fourth-quarter 2025 earnings on February 3rd. The company's performance is anticipated to be bolstered by new product launches, strategic partnerships, and strong microinverter shipments, despite expected softness in the European market and tariff-driven margin pressures on battery cell packs. Zacks analysts predict an earnings beat for ENPH, with an Earnings ESP of +6.54% and a Zacks Rank #3.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Waste Management misses Q4 2025 EPS forecast
feed.summary: Waste Management (WM) reported Q4 2025 earnings, missing EPS and revenue forecasts with an EPS of $1.93 against an anticipated $1.95, and revenue of $6.31 billion against $6.39 billion. Despite the misses, the company showed strong operational performance by improving its operating EBITDA margin by 40 basis points for the full year and increasing free cash flow by nearly 27% to $2.94 billion. Waste Management expects continued strong growth in 2026, with operating EBITDA projected to be between $8.15 billion and $8.25 billion, and free cash flow growth of nearly 30% to $3.8 billion, driven by operational excellence, sustainability investments, and integration of its Healthcare Solutions business.

feed.title: Tractor Supply reins in guidance after weak finish to the year
feed.summary: Tractor Supply missed its Q4 expectations with net sales up 3.3% and comps nearly flat, while net income dropped almost 4%. The weak performance was attributed to softer demand for discretionary goods, fewer severe weather events, and increased tariffs and promotional costs impacting margins. Consequently, the retailer has reined in its outlook for the current year, expecting more modest growth in sales and net income than analyst forecasts.

feed.title: Hexcel Corporation (NYSE:HXL) Increases Dividend to $0.18 Per Share
feed.summary: Hexcel Corporation (NYSE:HXL) has announced an increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.18 per share, up from $0.17, representing a 5.9% increase. The dividend will be paid on February 17th to shareholders of record on February 9th, with an annualized payout of $0.68 and a yield of 0.8%. The company recently reported strong earnings, beating analyst expectations with $0.52 EPS and $491.3 million in revenue, and its dividend is well-covered by earnings with a payout ratio of 27.0%.

feed.title: Blackstone-Backed Liftoff Mobile Seeks $762 Million in US IPO
feed.summary: Liftoff Mobile Inc., a company backed by Blackstone Inc. that specializes in mobile in-app advertising, is pursuing an initial public offering (IPO) aiming to raise up to $762 million. The company plans to offer 25.4 million shares at a price range of $26 to $30 per share. At the top end of this range, Liftoff Mobile would achieve a market valuation of $5.2 billion.

feed.title: Marcus & Millichap Completes Sale of Mixed-Use Property in Newtown Square near Philadelphia
feed.summary: Marcus & Millichap announced the sale of the Fieldstone Building, an 18,000-square-foot mixed-use property in Newtown Square, Pennsylvania, for over $2.1 million. The property, featuring 11 residential units and four commercial suites, drew significant market interest due to its location, existing cash flow, and development potential. Vince Peruto of Marcus & Millichap's Philadelphia office brokered the deal between the private seller and buyer.

feed.title: FICO's AI decision platform lands 2026 Gartner 'Leader' rating
feed.summary: FICO has been named a Leader in the 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Decision Intelligence Platforms, recognized for its Ability to Execute and Completeness of Vision. The company's FICO Platform, launched in 2019, is highlighted for empowering enterprises to make faster, smarter, and more explainable decisions using unified decisioning intelligence. This recognition underscores FICO's commitment to leveraging AI for real-world decision-making across various industries.

feed.title: Can Allstate Beat Q4 Earnings on Property-Liability Strength?
feed.summary: Allstate Corporation is expected to report its fourth-quarter 2025 results on February 4, 2026, with analysts forecasting a significant year-over-year jump in earnings and revenue. The company has a strong track record of beating earnings estimates, and its Q4 results are anticipated to benefit from growth in net premiums earned, higher net investment income, and improved underwriting income in Property-Liability, despite rising expenses.

feed.title: Lam Research Corp (NASDAQ:LRCX) Exceeds Market Expectations
feed.summary: Lam Research Corp (NASDAQ:LRCX) reported strong fiscal Q2 2026 results, surpassing market expectations for both earnings and revenue. The company achieved record revenues in 2025, with significant growth in its WFE and CSPG segments, driven by AI transformation. Looking ahead, Lam Research anticipates continued growth for Q1 2026, with revenue projected between $5.70 billion, plus or minus $300 million, exceeding consensus estimates.

feed.title: 5 Revealing Analyst Questions From Intuitive Surgical's Q4 Earnings Call
feed.summary: Intuitive Surgical exceeded Q4 expectations, driven by increased global procedure volumes and demand for its new da Vinci 5 system, particularly in international markets. The article highlights key analyst questions from the earnings call, focusing on FDA approval for cardiac procedures, ASC adoption strategies, gross margin dynamics, and addressing cost competition. Upcoming catalysts include the adoption of da Vinci 5, expansion into ASCs, and navigating competition and regulatory changes in international markets.

feed.title: Toyota becomes T-Mobile Arena’s first auto sponsor, with SUV giveaways
feed.summary: T-Mobile Arena has named Southern Nevada Toyota Dealers as its newest Founding Partner and first Official Automotive Sponsor. This multi-year agreement, facilitated by AEG Global Partnerships, will provide Toyota prominent brand visibility, including signage, vehicle displays, and naming rights to a VIP suite entrance. Fans can also look forward to interactive activations, exclusive giveaways like a 4Runner sweepstakes, and community-driven initiatives.

feed.title: Inside HOKA's lightest marathon shoe yet: the $275 Cielo X1 3.0
feed.summary: HOKA, a division of Deckers Brands, has launched the Cielo X1 3.0, its lightest super shoe designed for race day. This new model focuses on minimizing weight and maximizing performance through features like a carbon fiber plate, responsive PEBA foam, and a lightweight, breathable design. Elite HOKA athletes have already tested and achieved notable results with the shoe in major marathons and half marathons.

feed.title: WEC Energy Group to announce 2025 fourth-quarter and year-end financial results Feb. 5
feed.summary: WEC Energy Group (NYSE: WEC) will release its 2025 fourth-quarter and full-year earnings before the stock market opens on Thursday, Feb. 5, 2026. A conference call for investors and security analysts is scheduled for the same day at 1 p.m. Central time, with detailed financial information available on the company's website by 6:30 a.m. Central time. The company, based in Milwaukee, serves 4.7 million customers across Wisconsin, Illinois, Michigan, and Minnesota.

feed.title: A.O. Smith Q4 & FY 2025 slides: Record EPS despite China headwinds
feed.summary: A.O. Smith Corporation reported record adjusted earnings per share of $3.85 for full-year 2025, a 6% increase from the previous year, despite facing headwinds in its Chinese operations. The company's North America segment was the primary growth driver, and it strategically acquired Leonard Valve to expand its water management reach. A.O. Smith projects continued growth and shareholder returns for 2026, though challenges in China are expected to persist.

feed.title: Sherwin-Williams Q4 2025 slides: Sales up 5.6%, adjusted EPS rises 6.7%
feed.summary: Sherwin-Williams reported solid financial results for Q4 2025 and the full year, with quarterly sales rising 5.6% and adjusted EPS increasing by 6.7%. Despite this positive performance, the stock saw a pre-market decline of 2.75%. The company provided a 2026 outlook, anticipating low to mid-single-digit consolidated sales growth and adjusted EPS guidance between $11.50 and $11.90, emphasizing proactive strategies to navigate challenging market conditions.

feed.title: Packaging Corporation of America (NYSE:PKG) Given New $227.00 Price Target at Citigroup
feed.summary: Citigroup has raised its price target for Packaging Corporation of America (NYSE:PKG) to $227.00 from $226.00, maintaining a "neutral" rating. Other analysts have also recently adjusted their price targets and ratings for PKG, resulting in a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating and a target price of $236.00. The company recently reported Q4 earnings that missed analyst estimates and provided Q1 2026 guidance.

feed.title: Take-Two Interactive Software (NASDAQ:TTWO) Given Outperform Rating at Wedbush
feed.summary: Wedbush analysts have reaffirmed an "outperform" rating for Take-Two Interactive Software (NASDAQ:TTWO), setting a target price of $300.00, suggesting a potential upside of 26.16%. This latest rating follows a mix of other analyst reports, with a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating and an average price target of $273.11 for the company. The article also details recent insider stock sales and institutional trading activity.

feed.title: Assessing Broadridge Financial Solutions (BR) After Recent Share Price Weakness
feed.summary: Broadridge Financial Solutions (BR) has experienced recent share price weakness, with a 7.1% decline year-to-date, though long-term returns remain positive. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is undervalued by 33.8%, while its P/E ratio, though above industry average, is slightly below Simply Wall St's "Fair Ratio," also indicating it may be slightly undervalued. The article encourages investors to use "Narratives" for personalized valuation based on future expectations.

feed.title: Landstar System (NASDAQ:LSTR) Price Target Cut to $145.00 by Analysts at Truist Financial
feed.summary: Truist Financial analysts have reduced their price target for Landstar System (NASDAQ:LSTR) from $150.00 to $145.00, maintaining a "hold" rating on the transportation company's stock. This new target suggests a potential downside of 4.50% from the current price. Other analysts have varying ratings and price targets, with the consensus being a "Hold" and an average price target of $152.58.

feed.title: Does FIS’s New Tech-Focused Director Appointment Refine Its Core Banking and Payments Strategy?
feed.summary: Fidelity National Information Services (FIS) recently appointed Anil Chakravarthy to its Board of Directors, a move aimed at bolstering its focus on core banking and payments technology. Chakravarthy's background in cloud, subscription software, and enterprise security aligns with FIS's strategy to lean into software-driven models and improve operational discipline and product innovation. While this appointment reinforces the board's technology credentials, its immediate impact on FIS's short-term challenges like margin recovery and balance sheet strength is expected to be limited, with the company's valuation still indicating a potentially expensive stock.

feed.title: Eagle Materials (NYSE:EXP) Announces Earnings Results
feed.summary: Eagle Materials (NYSE:EXP) reported Q3 earnings per share of $3.22, missing analyst estimates of $3.39, with revenue of $555.96 million. The company's revenue was down 0.4% year-over-year, and EPS saw a 10% decline driven by lower wallboard volumes. Despite underperformance in light materials, heavy materials like cement and aggregate showed strong growth, and management announced cement price increases for early 2026 and robust capital allocation plans.

feed.title: Willdan Group stock hits all-time high at 135.27 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Willdan Group's stock reached an all-time high of $135.27, marking a 259% increase over the past year. This growth follows strong Q3 2025 financial results, the acquisition of Compass Municipal Advisors, and a significant $97 million contract with Alameda County for energy upgrades. Wedbush also raised its price target for the company to $145 with an Outperform rating.

feed.title: IBM’s A3 ratings affirmed by Moody’s, outlook changed to stable
feed.summary: Moody's Ratings has affirmed International Business Machines Corporation's (NYSE:IBM) A3 senior unsecured rating and changed its outlook from negative to stable, citing strong operating performance in 2025. The agency expects IBM's revenue and EBITDA growth to exceed 5% and 12% respectively in 2026, with gross leverage projected to decline. IBM's strong liquidity and focus on acquisitions for hybrid cloud growth are also key factors in the stable outlook.

feed.title: LG Electronics upgraded to Baa1 by Moody’s on improved metrics
feed.summary: Moody’s Ratings has upgraded LG Electronics Inc.'s issuer and senior unsecured ratings to Baa1 from Baa2, with a stable outlook. This upgrade reflects significant improvements in LG Display’s operating performance and reduced debt leverage, minimizing the financial support needed from LG Electronics. Both LG Display and LG Electronics have shown improved debt-to-EBITDA ratios, supported by debt reductions, asset sales, and a strategic shift towards high-margin OLED products.

feed.title: Three Predictions for Sovereign Cloud in 2026
feed.summary: In this article, Dave Michels makes three predictions for the European sovereign cloud market in 2026. He anticipates that US hyperscalers will continue to dominate the market despite increasing demand for European alternatives, due to switching costs and the need for US software. Michels also expects ongoing debate and lack of agreement on the definition of 'sovereign cloud' will persist, leading to compromise solutions and national policies. Finally, he predicts that European organizations, particularly in the public sector, will actively experiment with diverse sovereign cloud solutions, driven by geopolitical concerns and the need for independence from foreign governments.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Tractor Supply Q4 2025 misses forecasts, stock dips
feed.summary: Tractor Supply (TSCO) reported disappointing Q4 2025 earnings, missing EPS and revenue forecasts, leading to a 4.33% stock drop in pre-market trading. Despite challenges like reduced discretionary spending and a quiet storm season impacting sales, the company achieved a 4.3% increase in full-year net sales and plans to open 100 new stores and expand strategic initiatives in 2026. Management expressed optimism for 2026, anticipating a normalization of business and a return to long-term growth targets.

feed.title: Blue Owl Capital Shareholder Class Action Filed
feed.summary: A shareholder class action lawsuit has been filed against Blue Owl Capital (NYSE: OWL) alleging misrepresentations regarding liquidity issues between February 6 and November 16, 2025. Shareholders have until February 2, 2026, to file papers to serve as lead plaintiff. Bernstein Liebhard LLP, a firm with a strong track record and contingency fee structure, is representing the shareholders in this action.

feed.title: Hubbell Incorporated Showcases Grid Automation Breakthroughs At DTECH 2026
feed.summary: Hubbell Incorporated is set to present its latest innovations in grid automation and intelligent infrastructure at DTECH 2026, featuring its new Aclara360 software. This modular system helps utilities utilize advanced grid analytics for smarter network decisions without significant capital investment. The company will also highlight the successful pilot program for its LineDefender recloser with 36 utilities across North America.

feed.title: Adp stock hits 52-week low at 247.15 usd By Investing.com
feed.summary: Automatic Data Processing Inc. (ADP) stock has fallen to a new 52-week low of $247.15, marking a significant downturn with a 14.75% decrease over the past year. Despite its impressive dividend history, the stock's valuation appears high given its growth prospects, though InvestingPro analysis suggests it might be undervalued. This comes as ADP reported strong Q2 2026 earnings but received adjusted, lower price targets from analysts due to a modest growth outlook.

feed.title: U.S. Bank CEO Kedia named chair
feed.summary: U.S. Bank CEO Gunjan Kedia will become the chair, consolidating the CEO and chair roles, a move that aligns U.S. Bank with all other eight U.S.-based global systemically important banks. Kedia's predecessor, Andy Cecere, is retiring from the board. Roland Hernandez will remain the lead independent director, a role often maintained to balance the power of a combined CEO-chair.

feed.title: Wolfe Research Adjusts Price Target on Host Hotels & Resorts to $21 From $19, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: Wolfe Research has increased its price target for Host Hotels & Resorts (HST) to $21 from $19, while reaffirming an Outperform rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects an updated positive outlook from the firm regarding the specialized REIT. The article also lists several other recent analyst price target adjustments and ratings for Host Hotels & Resorts.

feed.title: Elevance Health posts Q4 gains
feed.summary: Elevance Health reported fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 financial results meeting expectations but signaling future challenges, including a projected decrease in 2026 EPS. The company saw a 10% increase in Q4 operating revenue and strengthened its earnings power through pricing discipline, despite a decline in medical membership due to Medicaid attrition and higher medical cost trends. Elevance Health aims to return to at least 12% adjusted EPS growth by 2027 and returned $4.1 billion to shareholders in 2025.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Southwest Airlines Q4 2025 sees EPS beat, stock surges
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines reported Q4 2025 earnings with an EPS of $0.58, exceeding the forecast of $0.57, though revenue of $7.4 billion fell short of the $7.5 billion expectation. Despite the revenue miss, the company's stock surged 6.22% in premarket trading due to strong future guidance and operational achievements, including a projected 2026 adjusted EPS of at least $4. The call highlighted successful implementation of new initiatives like assigned seating, extra legroom, and expanded online presence, driving optimism for continued growth and profitability.

feed.title: Huntington Bancshares stock slips as $1.75 billion note sale adds focus on Cadence deal financing
feed.summary: Huntington Bancshares' stock slipped after the bank announced a $1.75 billion debt offering, drawing investor scrutiny on its balance-sheet strategy ahead of the Cadence Bank acquisition. The debt sale, comprising senior and subordinated notes, is intended to support Huntington's projected growth in 2026, which is partly tied to the Cadence merger. Investors are keenly watching the integration process and the impact of interest rate changes on bank margins.

feed.title: PulteGroup Sounds Cautious On Demand Even As Home Affordability Improves
feed.summary: PulteGroup (PHM) reported mixed fourth-quarter results, beating analyst expectations for adjusted earnings and revenue. Despite improved home affordability due to lower interest rates, the homebuilder noted that lagging consumer confidence continued to impact homebuyer demand. The company remains focused on asset management, cash flow generation, and developing a land pipeline to support future community count growth.

feed.title: Salesforce Hits Fresh 52-Week Low As Microsoft, SAP, ServiceNow Earnings Jolt Software Stocks
feed.summary: Salesforce (NYSE: CRM) shares have dropped to a new 52-week low following recent earnings reports from Microsoft, SAP, and ServiceNow, which have created a risk-off sentiment in the software sector. Concerns about cloud demand, deal momentum, and AI return on investment timelines have been reignited, negatively impacting Salesforce's stock which trades as a key enterprise software indicator. The company is experiencing a bearish trend, with its stock trading significantly below its moving averages, and is expected to report its next earnings on February 25th.

feed.title: Is Corteva (CTVA) Pricing Fully Reflects Food Security Tailwinds And Recent Share Gains
feed.summary: Corteva's stock performance has shown significant gains across various periods, yet a valuation analysis by Simply Wall St suggests it may be overvalued. Both a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model and a Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio comparison indicate that Corteva is trading at a premium, with the DCF model pointing to a 15.4% overvaluation and the P/E ratio exceeding industry and peer averages as well as Simply Wall St's proprietary Fair Ratio. Investors are encouraged to use "Narratives" to incorporate their own views and future expectations into valuation models to make informed decisions.

feed.title: Assessing Crown Castle (CCI) Valuation After Analyst Revisions Following DISH Wireless Default
feed.summary: Crown Castle (CCI) is undergoing a re-evaluation by analysts following DISH Wireless's default on payment obligations, prompting KeyBanc to adjust its models. Despite recent share price declines, CCI is trading at a significant discount according to Simply Wall St's narrative, driven by its strategic decision to sell its fiber segment and focus on being a pure-play U.S. tower company. The undervaluation is based on expectations of improved operational excellence, customer service, and profitability, supported by consistent growth in U.S. mobile data demand and 5G deployments.
